---
title: "Original research (reproducible sub-projects)"
root: ".."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

This hub site is designed so each original research deliverable lives in its **own GitHub repository** (reproducible, with its own README, data dictionary, and—if applicable—code + environment specification).

Update the links below once you create those repositories.

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
### 1) Open documentation: AD datasets catalog
Create an open documentation to recap all available AD datasets and their content (as exhaustive as possible).

- **Format:** GitHub repository + issues for community contributions
- **Recommended structure:** data dictionary, modality tags, access requirements, geographies, demographics reporting checklist

[Repo link](https://github.com/KahinaBch/ad-datasets-catalog){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.card}
### 2) Open documentation: shared atlases & harmonisation pipelines
Create an open documentation listing shared atlases and harmonisation pipelines (neuroimaging + multimodal).

- **Format:** GitHub repository + versioned inventory
- **Recommended fields:** modality, license, citation, scope, input/output, validation benchmarks

[Repo link](https://github.com/KahinaBch/ad-atlases-harmonisation){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.card}
### 3) Reproducibility study: code/data sharing in *Alzheimer’s & Dementia*
Investigate the proportion of papers sharing code and datasets in the journal.

- **Format:** reproducible repo (data extraction protocol + analysis scripts + outputs)
- **Include:** PRISMA-like flow, inter-rater reliability, open dataset of metadata where permitted

[Repo link](https://github.com/KahinaBch/ad-reproducibility-study){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

---

## Link back

- [Interactive Explorer](../part2/explorer.qmd)
